short_def: Documenting the real process of planning, building, and measuring a website or product in the open, including the decisions, mistakes, and results, instead of only sharing a polished final result.
definition:
Building in public means recording and sharing the actual work as it happens: the planning, the false starts, the architecture decisions, the AI prompts, the SEO experiments, and the results, good or bad. On AI Now Guide, this takes the form of channels and sessions: a channel groups a body of related work (a site rebuild, a research thread, a workflow), and each session is a single recorded working block within that channel.

The goal is not performance. It is a transparent record that is useful to the person doing the work and to anyone else trying to do something similar. A build-in-public archive becomes its own reference library over time, because every session is dated, scoped, and tied back to a channel.
example: A channel like "Website Rebuilds" might contain a dozen sessions, each one a single recorded sitting where a specific page, template, or migration step gets built. Someone following along can watch session three of that channel and see exactly what changed, why, and what broke along the way, without having to guess at the backstory.
nuance: Building in public is not the same as building carelessly. The sessions and channels on this site are still organized: each one has a title, a description, and a place in the taxonomy. The transparency is structured, which is what makes it useful as a reference later instead of just a stream of unsorted clips.

seo.title: Build in Public | AI Now Guide Glossary
seo.description: What "build in public" means on AI Now Guide: documenting the real process behind channels and sessions, not just the finished result.
